opening_hook.text: A 5W green laser (532nm) is being set up in a lab. At what distance does the beam become 'safe' to view? The answer requires understanding NOHD.

core_concept.title: What is NOHD?
core_concept.text: NOHD (Nominal Ocular Hazard Distance) is the distance from the laser at which the beam irradiance equals the Maximum Permissible Exposure (MPE). Beyond this distance, the beam is considered eye-safe.
key_points: NOHD marks the boundary of the eye hazard zone
Inside NOHD: Direct beam viewing exceeds MPE (hazardous)
Outside NOHD: Beam intensity is below MPE (safe for brief exposure)
NOHD depends on laser power, beam divergence, and MPE

visual_demonstration.description: Interactive slider showing how beam spreads with distance

steps.title: Select Wavelength
steps.instruction: Choose your laser wavelength to determine the correct MPE

steps.title: Enter Laser Power
steps.instruction: Enter the output power in Watts or milliwatts

steps.title: Enter Beam Divergence
steps.tooltip: Typical values: 0.5-5 mrad for most lasers

steps.title: Determine MPE
steps.instruction: Based on wavelength and exposure time, the MPE is calculated

steps.title: Calculate NOHD
steps.instruction: Review the calculation and compute NOHD

content.title: Understanding NHZ and Controlled Zones
content.description: Visualize the complete safety zone hierarchy for a laser installation

zones.name: Nominal Hazard Zone (NHZ)
zones.definition: Space within which the level of direct, reflected, or scattered radiation exceeds the applicable MPE

zones.name: Controlled Zone (CZ)
zones.definition: Area where laser radiation in excess of MPE may be present, but only authorized personnel may enter

zones.name: Laser Controlled Area (LCA)
zones.definition: Area where Class 3B or Class 4 laser radiation exceeds MPE and engineering controls are required

problems.scenario: A 2W green laser (532nm) with 1.5 mrad divergence is being installed.

questions.text: What is the MPE for this scenario?
questions.hint_1: For visible light, 18μs < t < 10s: MPE = 1.8 × t^0.75 mW/cm²
questions.hint_2: Calculate: 1.8 × (0.25)^0.75 = 1.8 × 0.354 = 0.637... Wait, let me recalculate for visible light correctly.
questions.hint_3: For 532nm, the MPE formula gives 2.54 mW/cm² for 0.25s exposure (blink reflex)

questions.hint_1: Use the formula: NOHD = (1/θ) × √(1.27 × P / MPE)
questions.hint_2: First convert: P = 2W = 2000 mW, MPE = 2.54 mW/cm²
